name: Cabo Verde

visa_free.notes: Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. Listed on the official gov.cv page as 180 days. Ordinary passport: 90 days; up to 180 days with official document proving official service mission or business travel (Lei n.º 18/II/82, Decretos n.º 7/95, 106/85 e 24/97). Diplomatic and Service passports: 180 days.
Visa-free entry; 90-day limit (ECOWAS member). Listed as 'Costa do Marfim' on the Portuguese version of the official page.
Protocolo de Livre Circulação de Pessoas (ECOWAS) and Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. 180 days for diplomatic/service/ordinary passports.
Visa-free entry; 30-day limit. Listed as 'Holanda' on the Portuguese version of the official page.
Visa-free entry; 30-day limit. Listed as 'Hong Kong' (SAR) on the official page.
Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. Ordinary passport: up to 90 days; up to 180 days with official document proving service mission. Diplomatic, Service and Special passports: 180 days.

Ordinary passport: 30 days per Resolução n.º 134/2018. Diplomatic, Special and Service passports: indefinite stay (Acordo Bilateral + CPLP). The official MNEC PDF states 'Indeterminado' (indefinite) for Diplomatic/Special/Service passport holders.

Visa-free entry; 90 days. Listed as 'Rússia' on the official gov.cv visa-exempt page. Ordinary passport holders are visa-free. Based on the bilateral agreement Decreto n.º 8/95, de 28 de agosto (originally diplomatic/service passports); the gov.cv page confirms ordinary passport holders are now included in the exemption.
Acordo no âmbito da CPLP. Ordinary passport: 90 days; up to 180 days with official document proving service mission. Diplomatic, Service and Special passports: 180 days.
Visa-free entry; 30-day limit. Listed as 'Vaticano' on the official page.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Nationals of countries without Cabo Verde diplomatic representation
visa_on_arrival.notes: Citizens residing in countries where Cabo Verde has no diplomatic representation may apply for visas upon entry at the borders per portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv. Note: as of January 1, 2026, Cabo Verde requires prior entry visas for 96 nationalities (see EASE official notice: ease.gov.cv/assets/lista_de_paises.pdf). This border-on-arrival exception applies only to those without access to a Cabo Verde consulate.

e_visa.notes: Cabo Verde operates an online pre-arrival visa application system via portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv. Tourist visa allows up to 90 days stay (extendable once for additional 90 days). All travelers (including visa-free) must also pre-register via ease.gov.cv and pay the Airport Security Fee (TSA, approximately EUR 30.83 / CVE 3,400 for international flights) prior to arrival.

visa_policy.notes: The official gov.cv page (paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde) lists 59 visa-exempt nationalities with specific stay limits, confirmed by the official MNEC consular portal PDF (paisesisentosvistos.pdf). All foreign travelers must pre-register via ease.gov.cv up to 5 days before travel and pay an Airport Security Fee (TSA: CVE 3,400 / approx EUR 30.83 for international flights; CVE 150 for inter-island). As of January 1, 2026, Cabo Verde requires prior visas for 96 nationalities listed in the official EASE notice (ease.gov.cv/assets/lista_de_paises.pdf) per Decree No. 244/GMAI/2025, including Afghanistan, Algeria, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Belarus, Bolivia, Botswana, Brunei, Burundi, Bhutan, Cambodia, Cameroon, Chad, Chile, Colombia, Comoros, Congo, North Korea, Costa Rica, Djibouti, Dominica, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Eritrea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Philippines, Gabon, Guatemala, Guyana, Haiti, Honduras, India, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Jamaica, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kiribati, Laos, Lesotho, Lebanon, Libya, Madagascar, Malawi, Mauritania, Mexico, Mongolia, Myanmar, Namibia, Nauru, Nepal, Nicaragua, Oman, Palestine, Palau, Panama, Papua New Guinea, Pakistan, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Kyrgyzstan, Central African Republic, Democratic Republic of Congo, Dominican Republic, Rwanda, Samoa, Syria, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Sudan, South Sudan, Suriname, Taiwan, Tajikistan, Tanzania, Tonga, Tokelau, Trinidad and Tobago, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Tuvalu, Uganda, Uzbekistan, Vanuatu, Venezuela, Vietnam, Yemen, Zambia, Zimbabwe (96 total). The official list does not include USA, Canada, Japan, Australia, or several other countries that some third-party sources indicate may enter visa-free; these could not be confirmed on the official gov.cv page. Cuba is listed on the official gov.cv page as 'durante a missão' (during the mission) which refers to the bilateral diplomatic/official mission arrangement (Decreto n.º 79/82) and applies to diplomatic/service/official passport holders only — not ordinary tourists.

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (96 countries per Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they hold a valid residence authorization (autorização de residência válida) issued by an EU Member State, Iceland, Liechtenstein, San Marino, Switzerland, or Vatican. Verbatim: 'titulares de autorizações de residência válidas emitidas por Estados-Membros da União Europeia, Islândia, Liechtenstein, Reino Unido, San Marino, Suíça e Vaticano'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause; traveller enters without needing a prior visa.

Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (96 countries per Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they hold a valid residence authorization (autorização de residência válida) issued by the United Kingdom. Verbatim Portuguese: 'titulares de autorizações de residência válidas emitidas por ... Reino Unido'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

credential.subtype: any valid residence document guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (96 countries per Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they are legal residents in the United States, provided the document guarantees unconditional readmission. Verbatim: 'residentes legais no Brasil, Canadá e Estados Unidos da América, desde que o documento garanta a readmissão incondicional do titular nesses países'. This covers US permanent residents (Green Card holders) and other legal resident categories. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

credential.subtype: any valid residence document guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (96 countries per Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they are legal residents in Canada, provided the document guarantees unconditional readmission. Verbatim: 'residentes legais no Brasil, Canadá e Estados Unidos da América, desde que o documento garanta a readmissão incondicional do titular nesses países'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

credential.subtype: any valid residence document guaranteeing unconditional readmission

conditional_access.conditions: Nationals of countries on the prior-visa-required list (96 countries per Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025) are exempt from the prior visa requirement if they are legal residents in Brazil, provided the document guarantees unconditional readmission. Verbatim: 'residentes legais no Brasil, Canadá e Estados Unidos da América, desde que o documento garanta a readmissão incondicional do titular nesses países'. No max stay stated in the exemption clause.

Holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports of any country are exempt from the prior visa requirement and may receive diplomatic, official, or courtesy visas. Despacho 244/GMAI/2025 states: holders of diplomatic or service passports, as well as citizens traveling by invitation of Cabo Verde authorities, receive differentiated treatment (diplomatic, official, or courtesy visas). Pre-existing bilateral agreements: Cuba — Decreto n.º 79/82, de 28 de agosto (diplomatic/service/official passports, duration of mission); Russia — Decreto n.º 8/95, de 28 de agosto (diplomatic and service passports, 90 days, though Russia ordinary passport holders are now also visa-free per the gov.cv exempt list).

Decreto n.º 79/82, de 28 de agosto. Diplomatic, Service and Official passports only; duration is 'during the mission' (durante a missão). The gov.cv visa-exempt page lists Cuba as 'durante a missão' (during the mission) — this refers to the bilateral diplomatic/official mission arrangement, not ordinary tourist access. Ordinary passport holders from Cuba require a visa.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program was found on any official Cabo Verde government website (gov.cv, mnec.gov.cv, e-portaldef.gov.cv, mf.gov.cv). The Green Card is a permanent residence permit for real estate investors, not a citizenship program. Third-party/non-official sources mention investment-linked routes to citizenship but these were not confirmed on official government pages.

programs.name: Green Card - Autorização de Residência Permanente (Real Estate Investment)
programs.notes: A permanent residence permit granted to foreigners who purchase built or under-construction real estate worth at least EUR 80,000 (CVE 8,821,200) in municipalities with per-capita GDP below the national average, OR at least EUR 120,000 (CVE 13,231,800) in municipalities with per-capita GDP at or above the national average. Investment must be paid with funds transferred from abroad. Authorized by Lei nº 30/IX/2018 (creates the differentiated status for second-residence holders via Green Card) and Decreto-Regulamentar nº 1/2020 (establishes the implementing regulations). Application portal at e-portaldef.gov.cv. The Green Card grants permanent residence directly. Both the law and regulation are listed on the AAI (Alta Autoridade para a Imigração) official documentation portal at aai.gov.cv.

programs.name: Autorização de Residência - Actividade de Investimento ou Actividade Económica Relevante
programs.notes: Temporary Residence Permit for foreigners carrying out investment activity or relevant economic activity in Cabo Verde. No minimum investment amount is stated on the official page. Requires valid entry visa, criminal record certificate, medical certificate, international vaccination certificate, Social Security registration, proof of accommodation, and documentation of the investment activity (authorization/registration by competent authority, External Investor Certificate, or proof of company employing at least 50% Cabo Verdean workers, or proof of activity of recognized national interest).

programs.name: Visto Nómadas Digitais (Digital Nomad Visa)
programs.eligibility: Persons working remotely for a company or entity outside Cabo Verde. Required documents: passport, criminal record from country of origin, proof of employment/contract with remote employer, proof of income, proof of accommodation in Cabo Verde, medical certificate.
programs.notes: Listed on the official National Police / e-Residencia portal (e-portaldef.gov.cv) under Visa types. The application portal was listed as 'Em manutenção' (under maintenance) at the time of research (June 2026). No duration or income threshold stated on the official page.

outbound_official.note: No official Cabo Verde government outbound visa-list page located. The official consular portal FAQ (portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv/en/perguntas-frequentes) explicitly states 'Os portadores do passaporte cabo-verdiano podem entrar sem visto, ou requerer visto à entrada, nos países constantes na lista Passport Index' and redirects citizens to passportindex.org — an aggregator — rather than maintaining its own government-published list of destinations where Cabo Verde ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa. The MNEC consular portal and gov.cv portals focus exclusively on inbound travel to Cabo Verde. Rely on destination-side data.

agreements: CPLP (Community of Portuguese Language Countries) special mobility agreement

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Russia (RUS) added to visa_policy.visa_free (90 days, ordinary passport). The previous record incorrectly placed Russia ONLY in conditional_access (diplomatic/service passport only per Decreto 8/95). The official gov.cv page (paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde) explicitly lists 'Rússia — 90 dias' among the 59 visa-exempt nationalities for ordinary passport holders. The conditional_access entry for Russia/diplomatic-service has been removed from conditional_access since the general diplomatic/service passport_type entry already covers it.

CORRECTION: Prior-visa-required country count corrected from '91' to '96' in notes and conditional_access conditions. The actual Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025 list (per EASE PDF ease.gov.cv/assets/lista_de_paises.pdf) contains 96 nationalities, not 91. Five countries were missing from the previous record's notes: Rwanda, Turkey, Taiwan, India, Cameroon.

CORRECTION: Source URL updated from 'https://www.gov.cv/en/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde' to 'https://www.gov.cv/paises-isentos-de-visto-para-cabo-verde' (removed '/en/' prefix which may not resolve reliably; Portuguese version is the primary official page).

CLARIFICATION: Cuba's 'durante a missão' listing on the gov.cv visa-exempt page is confirmed to apply only to diplomatic/service/official passport holders per Decreto n.º 79/82. Cuba ordinary passport holders require a visa. Cuba remains correctly in conditional_access only.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Angola (180 days) — the official gov.cv page confirms 180-day entry. MNEC PDF detail about 90-day ordinary / 180-day with mission document preserved in notes.

VERIFIED UNCHANGED: The 59 visa-free nationalities on the official gov.cv page are confirmed. No new countries (Canada, USA, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Morocco, Macao, Timor-Leste) appear on the official page.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: EASE pre-registration requirement and TSA fee (CVE 3,400 / approx EUR 30.83) confirmed still in effect as of June 2026.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: Green Card RBI program (EUR 80,000 minimum) and Digital Nomad Visa remain on official portals.
VERIFIED UNCHANGED: cbi.has_program = false confirmed — no citizenship-by-investment program found on official pages.

VERIFIED: India (IND) requires a prior visa for Cabo Verde — NOT visa-free or visa-on-arrival. India is explicitly named in the 96-country prior-visa-required list per Despacho n.º 244/GMAI/2025 (effective 1 Jan 2026). India does NOT appear in the official MNEC visa-exempt countries PDF (portalconsular.mnec.gov.cv/documents/20126/0/paisesisentosvistos.pdf). India has no entry in any visa_policy level (visa_free/visa_on_arrival/eta/e_visa) in this record, which is correct. Aggregator claim of visa-free or visa-on-arrival for India is FALSE per official Cabo Verde government source.
gaps: The official gov.cv visa-exempt list (59 entries) does not include USA, Canada, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Israel, Morocco, Macao, or Timor-Leste — these are not confirmed on any official Cabo Verde government page despite some third-party sources claiming visa-free access.

Russia: The exact legal basis for extending the existing Decreto 8/95 exemption (originally diplomatic/service only) to ordinary passport holders is not confirmed from the official gov.cv PDF (binary-inaccessible). The gov.cv page itself is the primary official confirmation of ordinary passport visa-free access.

Green Card RBI: Path to citizenship (if any) from Green Card permanent residence not stated on official page. Standard naturalization under Lei nº 66/VIII/2014 would apply but conditions not confirmed.
Green Card RBI: Exact processing time not stated on official page.
Investor residence permit (Actividade de Investimento): No minimum investment amount published on official page.
Digital Nomad visa: Income threshold, duration of stay, and processing time not stated on official page. Application portal listed as under maintenance at time of research (June 2026).
No official data found on fast-track programs for startups or highly skilled workers beyond the digital nomad visa.

Cuba visa exemption: Ordinary passport holders' exact requirements (visa-on-arrival vs. prior visa) not confirmed from official Cabo Verde government pages; third-party sources indicate visa-on-arrival. The 96-country prior-visa list does not include Cuba, suggesting Cuba ordinary passport holders may still get visa-on-arrival, but this is unconfirmed from official sources.

Conditional access — credential type precision: Despacho 244/GMAI/2025 uses the term 'residentes legais' (legal residents) for Brazil/Canada/US, and 'autorização de residência válida' (valid residence authorization) for EU/Iceland/Liechtenstein/UK/San Marino/Switzerland/Vatican. The decree does not specify individual permit subtypes. Full text could not be fetched directly from boe.incv.cv (PDF binary).
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption found on any official Cabo Verde source.
Diplomatic/service passport blanket waiver: max stay not confirmed per Despacho 244/GMAI/2025 text. Bilateral decrees for Cuba (Decreto 79/82) and Russia (Decreto 8/95) documented in the MNEC PDF.

The count of 96 countries in the prior-visa list is derived from third-party reporting on the EASE PDF (ease.gov.cv/assets/lista_de_paises.pdf); the PDF itself could not be parsed (binary format). The previous record cited 91 countries which appeared to be an undercount.
